reasoning: The Lean formalization uses ∃! to encode "there is a unique smallest topology". The property bound by ∃! asserts three things: (1) T' is a topology via the local `is_topology` definition (which correctly encodes univ ∈ T, binary intersections, arbitrary unions — with ∅ ∈ T following from empty union); (2) T' contains each T_i in the family via `∀ i, T i ⊆ T'`, matching the standard meaning of one topology containing another; (3) T' is smallest, via the universal property that any topology T'' containing all T_i satisfies T' ⊆ T''. This exactly matches "unique smallest topology containing all the collections T_α". Uniqueness is actually automatic from the smallest property (antisymmetry), so ∃! is equivalent to ∃ here, but this does not affect faithfulness. The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
